hero.headline: Chandigarh Online MBA in General Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Harvard and PwC certification options

sections.tldr: Chandigarh Online MBA in General Management covers corporate strategy, cross-cultural management, CSR, and M&A, with PwC-taught modules (premium tier), zero-cost direct university EMI, and 25% Early Bird scholarship at Rs 1,65,000. NIRF #19, NAAC A+. Ideal for generalist managers and career changers.

about.body:
General Management as an MBA specialisation is the broadest and most flexible track, suited for professionals who want comprehensive management capability without committing to a single functional area. It is particularly valuable for career changers, entrepreneurs, business owners, and mid-career managers transitioning from technical to leadership roles.

Chandigarh University Online's General Management MBA covers corporate strategy and policy, cross-cultural management, corporate social responsibility and sustainability, corporate mergers and acquisitions, entrepreneurship and innovation, and business analytics for managers. The curriculum equips graduates for P&L ownership, general manager roles, and leadership positions across all industries.

For Tier 2 enrollees, PwC's Management Consulting and Strategy advisory content is directly aligned with General Management - PwC works on corporate strategy, M&A advisory, business transformation, and sustainability consulting, all of which appear directly in the Chandigarh General Management syllabus.

Chandigarh University holds NIRF #19 overall and NIRF #32 in Management 2025, NAAC A+ accredited. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all Chandigarh MBA specialisations. See the main Chandigarh Online MBA page for core subject list.

subjects.name: Strategy, Business and Globalization
subjects.desc: Competitive strategy frameworks, industry analysis, and sustainable competitive advantage in global markets.

subjects.name: Decision Science
subjects.desc: Analytical decision-making tools for complex business problems and multi-criteria management decisions.

subjects.name: Corporate Strategy and Policy
subjects.desc: Corporate-level strategy, portfolio management, diversification decisions, and corporate governance.

subjects.name: Cross-Cultural Management
subjects.desc: Managing diverse teams across cultures, Hofstede frameworks, and international leadership.

subjects.name: Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainability
subjects.desc: CSR strategy, ESG reporting, stakeholder management, and integrating sustainability into business models.

subjects.name: Corporate Mergers and Acquisitions
subjects.desc: M&A strategy, deal valuation, due diligence, integration planning, and post-merger management.

subjects.name: Project Management
subjects.desc: General management of complex multi-functional projects and strategic initiative delivery.

subjects.name: AI for Managers
subjects.desc: AI strategy for general managers: automation decisions, AI governance, and workforce transition management.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship and Innovation
subjects.desc: Business model innovation, startup strategy, intrapreneurship frameworks, and venture development.

subjects.name: Business Analytics for Managers
subjects.desc: Data literacy for general managers: interpreting dashboards, analytics-driven decision-making, and KPI design.

subjects.name: Capstone Project II
subjects.desc: Strategic management project applied to a real business challenge with live industry partner.

whoHires.body:
General Management MBA graduates from Chandigarh University Online are hired across all industries and functions:

**Industries:** All sectors - from manufacturing and FMCG to IT, BFSI, consulting, real estate, and family businesses

**Entry-level roles:** Management Trainee, Business Development Executive, Strategy Analyst, Operations Coordinator, Junior Consultant

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** General Manager, Business Unit Head, Strategy Manager, Business Development Manager, Country Operations Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** COO, VP Business Development, Managing Director (regional or product), Partner (consulting), CEO (mid-size company)

**Top hiring organisations:** All sectors - TCS, Infosys (Management Consulting), Deloitte, KPMG Advisory, Amazon India, Flipkart, HDFC Bank, Reliance Industries, and family-owned businesses in manufacturing, trading, and services

skills.body:
The General Management specialisation builds broad strategic and leadership capability that general managers and business owners need:

**Technical skills:**
- Corporate Strategy: Porter's Five Forces, BCG matrix, Blue Ocean strategy, and strategic planning process design
- M&A Management: deal screening, valuation techniques (EV/EBITDA, DCF), data room management, and integration workstream planning
- Sustainability and CSR: SEBI BRSR framework, GRI standards, materiality assessment, and stakeholder engagement processes
- Business Analytics: dashboard interpretation, KPI hierarchy design, and data-driven business review facilitation

**Applied skills:**
- Cross-Cultural Management: managing multinational teams, adapting communication styles, and building inclusive team cultures
- Entrepreneurship: business model canvas, lean startup validation, pitch deck structure, and go-to-market planning

**Soft skills:** Running strategic planning offsite workshops with senior leadership, presenting business case proposals to boards, managing P&L accountability for a business unit, and making decisions with incomplete information under time pressure.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Chandigarh Online MBA General Management against peer programmes:

**vs Amity Online MBA General Management:** Amity's General Management programme is strong with NIRF #22 brand and a well-rounded curriculum. CU offers comparable NAAC A+ at Rs 1,65,000 Tier 1 (Amity is Rs 2,25,000) with the PwC Management Consulting and M&A advisory content (Tier 2) that Amity's programme does not offer at this co-teaching level.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA General Management:** NMIMS carries strong brand for General Management in BFSI and Mumbai-area consulting firms. Fee is higher at Rs 2,05,000-2,10,000. For candidates in Mumbai targeting financial services, NMIMS is the stronger brand. For North India professionals at a lower fee point, CU is the better value with PwC advisory content.

**vs Manipal Jaipur Online MBA General Management:** Both are NAAC A+ with similar fee profiles. CU differentiates through NIRF #19 and PwC Strategy and M&A advisory (Tier 2). For general managers wanting broader career optionality, CU's corporate M&A and sustainability curriculum is more current.

fees.body:
The Chandigarh University Online MBA in General Management offers 2 program tiers based on certification bundle:

**Tier 1 (Primary) - Standard with Harvard ManageMentor Certificate**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000
- 25% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,65,000 net payable (Rs 55,000 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 82,500 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 41,250 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,65,000 one-time
- Included certification: Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Tier 2 (Premium Upgrade) - 3-Certificate Bundle**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000 (same base fee)
- 18% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,80,400 net payable (Rs 39,600 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 90,200 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 45,100 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,80,400 one-time
- Included certifications (3):
  1. PwC co-created and co-taught MBA modules (unmatched in India)
  2. CAPM (Certified Associate in Project Management) certification preparation
  3. Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Additional charges (both tiers)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Chandigarh University (no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary, a differentiator among Indian online MBAs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options (6, 12, 18, or 24 months).

**Early Bird Scholarship terms**: Availability varies by admission cycle. The 25% Tier 1 scholarship and 18% Tier 2 scholarship are currently active but subject to timing. Reconfirm eligibility with our counsellor before enrolment.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Chandigarh University each quarter. Please reconfirm current tier options, Early Bird eligibility, and fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Chandigarh University MBA degree certificate with General Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 standards. NIRF #19 and NAAC A+ credentials are recognised by corporate employers, consulting firms, and government organisations.

Tier 1 graduates receive the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 graduates additionally receive PwC Management Consulting and Strategy co-teaching acknowledgement and CAPM preparation, useful for management consulting, M&A advisory, and strategy roles. DigiLocker verification is available.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Chandigarh University Online MBA General Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Sanjay M.
items.liked: Corporate M&A and PwC consulting frameworks gave me language for business development work
items.disliked: Business Analytics for Managers was basic compared to what the Analytics specialisation covers
items.body: I was a business development manager wanting to move into strategy. The Corporate M&A subject and PwC Strategy modules (Tier 2) gave me exactly the frameworks I needed. Cross-Cultural Management was directly applicable to my multinational team. Got promoted to Strategy Manager within 5 months. Harvard Business Publishing certificate was mentioned positively in my appraisal. Excellent ROI.

items.name: Divya K.
items.liked: NAAC A+ and NIRF #19 from a North India university was exactly what I needed for my career goals
items.disliked: Entrepreneurship subject could be more startup-ecosystem focused for aspiring founders
items.body: As a career changer from engineering to management, General Management gave me breadth across all business functions. CSR and Sustainability content was timely given my company's ESG reporting requirements. Zero-cost EMI through CU directly was smooth and straightforward. Recommend General Management for professionals who want strategic breadth rather than deep functional expertise.

items.name: Arun P.
items.liked: Zero-cost EMI and Harvard certificate both strong value additions
items.disliked: Tier selection process at enrolment was confusing - needs clearer online comparison tool
items.body: General Management was the right choice for me as a family business owner wanting formal management credentials. M&A and Corporate Strategy content helped me think about our business growth options. Harvard Business Publishing certificate added credibility in bank relationship meetings. CU's direct EMI made it affordable. Tier confusion at admission stage needs a better explanation process.

faqs.question: What is the PwC partnership in Chandigarh Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Chandigarh University's premium tier includes full MBA modules co-created and co-taught by PwC industry experts. This is unmatched among Indian online MBAs - most offer only generic curriculum. PwC-taught modules are particularly valuable for General Management professionals seeking roles at consulting firms, Big 4 advisory, or corporate strategy teams. The PwC partnership is included in Tier 2 (premium) at Rs 1,80,400 net.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Tier 1 and Tier 2 fee options?
faqs.answer: Both tiers share the same Rs 2,20,000 base fee and identical curriculum. Tier 1 applies a 25% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,65,000 net with the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 applies an 18% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,80,400 net with three certifications: Harvard, CAPM preparation, and PwC co-taught modules. For General Management professionals targeting consulting, M&A, or strategy roles, Tier 2's PwC content adds meaningful differentiation.

faqs.question: Is zero-cost EMI really available directly through Chandigarh University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Chandigarh University offers zero-cost EMI through the university's own payment infrastructure, not through an NBFC or external marketing partner. Tenures of 6, 12, 18, and 24 months are typically available. Confirm current options with our counsellor before enrolment.

faqs.question: Is General Management better than a functional specialisation for career flexibility?
faqs.answer: It depends on your career stage. General Management gives maximum flexibility and is best for: career changers, business owners, professionals who have already built functional depth and now need management breadth, and those targeting P&L or general manager roles. Functional specialisations (Finance, Marketing, Analytics) are better for professionals who want deep expertise in one domain and hire into specialist tracks. If you are unsure, our counsellor can help you map your career goals to the right choice.

faqs.question: Does the MBA cover family business succession and governance?
faqs.answer: The General Management specialisation includes Corporate Strategy and Policy and Corporate Mergers and Acquisitions, which provide frameworks applicable to family business succession, governance restructuring, and inorganic growth. The Entrepreneurship and Innovation subject also covers business model renewal. Chandigarh University separately offers a Family Business specialisation for professionals who want dedicated focus on family firm governance. Consider that specialisation if family business management is your primary goal.

chooseIf: You are a career changer, entrepreneur, or business owner wanting broad management credentials at NIRF #19, NAAC A+.
You want PwC Strategy and M&A consulting content (Tier 2) alongside a flexible management degree.
You are targeting general manager, P&L owner, or business development leadership roles.
You prefer zero-cost EMI directly through the university.
consider: You need NMIMS or IIM brand recognition for management consulting firm hiring.
You have a specific functional career path - a specialist MBA (Finance, Analytics, HR) will add more targeted value.
Your budget is under Rs 1.2 lakh and LPU NAAC A++ at lower fee is acceptable.
